meta.source: Shaare Zedek Medical Center — IV Drug Protocols Binder
meta.approved_by: Dr. Deborah Sarah Shapira (32398), Head of Geriatrics Department, SZMC
meta.note: All high-risk drugs require double-check (בקרה כפולה) by a second nurse

protocols.authors: Dr. Yigal Halbitz (writer), Chaya Broyar (editor), Dr. Philip Levin (approver)

general_guidelines.prerequisite: Physician order documented in patient chart before starting
general_guidelines.indication: Patient fed enterally, parenterally, or receiving IV fluids with glucose

monitoring.during_titration: Blood glucose every 2 hours until target reached
monitoring.after_rate_change_or_bolus: At least 2 checks, minimum 2 hours apart, until glucose balanced

rows.all_columns: STOP insulin. Give IV DEXTROSE 50% 50ml slow push over 2-5 min (physician order). Check glucose every 15 min until glucose stable above 140. When >140 restart insulin per appropriate column.
If >400 on 2 consecutive checks, move to 'high insulin requirement' column. Notify physician immediately.

protocols.title_he: הנחיות למתן פרופופול 2% בווריד — הרדמה למטופל מונשם
protocols.title_en: IV Propofol 2% — Sedation for Ventilated Patients

preparation.line_setup: Via Y-set with Fentanyl or dedicated IV line; do NOT use same line as propofol without Y-set

dosing.induction: NOT used for induction in ward — only for maintenance sedation
dosing.titration: Increase by 5-10 mcg/kg/min every 10 minutes until target sedation
dosing.target_sedation: Ramsay score 3-5 or per physician order
dosing.max_duration_note: Risk of PRIS increases significantly >48 hours

infusion_rate_table.description: Infusion rate drip (mL/hr) of Propofol 2% (20 mg/ml) by body weight

pris_warning.name: Propofol-Related Infusion Syndrome (PRIS)
pris_warning.risk_factors: Dose >50 mcg/kg/min, duration >48 hours, catecholamine use, critical illness

monitoring.adjusted_body_weight: Use BMI ≤30 for dosing; for obese patients use Adjusted Body Weight

pris_warning.action_if_suspected: Stop propofol immediately. Notify physician. Supportive care. Consider alternative sedation (Dormicum/Midazolam).

additional_notes: Change syringe every 12 hours (infection risk — lipid emulsion)
Do NOT mix with other drugs in same syringe
Propofol is NOT suitable for non-ventilated patients in ward
IV continuous infusion — max 3 days preferred
Must use aseptic technique; 2% propofol formulation has 25 mg/ml

loading_dose.repeat: May repeat if severe pain without significant side effects, after peak effect (~5 minutes from administration)

maintenance.titration: May increase every 30-60 minutes until clinical effect and pain control

steps: Take 1 ampoule of Fentanyl (0.5 mg / 10 ml)
Add 40 ml of NaCl 0.9%
Final: 50 ml total at 10 mcg/ml concentration
Take 50 ml bag of NaCl 0.9%
Remove 10 ml from the bag
Insert 1 ampoule of Fentanyl (0.5 mg / 10 ml)
Set IVAC rate per physician order

protocols.title_he: הוראות למתן IV FENTANYL — הכנה ללילה

preparation.total_ampoules: 21 ampoules needed for dilution = 100 mcg total per ml (check)
steps: NACL 0.9% 100cc — remove 20 ml
Add FENTANYL 20 ml (from ampoules)
Final volume: 100 ml. Ratio: 1 ml = 1 mcg (verify per unit)

protocols.note: High-risk drug — requires double-check (בקרה כפולה) by second nurse

steps: Take 100 ml NaCl 0.9%, remove 20 ml (leaving 80 ml)
Add Dormicum (100 mg total) = 20 ml of concentrate
Final: 100 mg in 100 ml = 1 mg/ml

protocols.administration: Rate per physician order. High-risk drug — double-check required.

protocols.availability: Available in MAO pharmacy during hours (14 hrs); otherwise contact specific unit

steps: D5% or NaCl — 100 ml base, remove volume as needed
Add 5 ampoules of Dopamine
DOPAMIN 200mg/5ml per ampoule, add ampoule contents

protocols.administration: Rate per physician order. IV pump required.

steps: Take 2 bags NaCl 0.9% (total volume needed)
Add HEPARIN ampoules to achieve 100 units/ml
Final concentration: 100 units per 1 ml
preparation.note: Exact volumes depend on total units ordered — follow physician instructions (IV)

contraindications: DIC (Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation) — relative, depending on clinical context

clinical: Check for hematomas at injection/IV sites
Monitor for signs of HIT (platelet drop >50% from baseline)
Watch for retroperitoneal, GI, or intracranial bleeding

special_notes: Vitreous body hemorrhage risk in specific patient populations
Target aPTT per institutional protocol (typically 1.5-2.5x control)
Protamine reversal: 1 mg per 100 units heparin given in last 2-3 hours

protocols.notes: All doses per standard infectious disease guidelines. Blood cultures x3 before starting antibiotics. Echocardiography (TTE then TEE if needed). Consult infectious disease.

steps: Remove 5 ml from a 100 ml NaCl 0.9% bag
Add 5 ml of MORPHINE (100 mg total)
Final: 100 mg in 100 ml = 1 mg/ml

protocols.administration: Rate per physician order. Continuous infusion or bolus as prescribed.
protocols.safety: High-risk drug — requires double-check (בקרה כפולה) by additional nurse.

loading.repeat: May repeat once after 5 minutes if no response

maintenance.infusion_ml_hr_by_dose: Approximately 15-60 ml/hr depending on weight and target dose
maintenance.typical_range: 1B standard: 45-90 mg/hr (approximately 1.5-2 ml/hr adjusted by weight)

indications: Ventricular arrhythmias (VT, VF refractory to standard ACLS)
protocols.monitoring: Continuous cardiac monitoring. Watch for CNS toxicity signs. Check lidocaine levels if prolonged use.

preparation.ampoule: Magnesium Sulfate 50% 20 ml (= 4 ampoules of 5 ml, or as available)
steps: Add MgSO4 to 100 ml NaCl 0.9%
Infuse over approximately 2 hours
May repeat per physician order — check Mg level first

clinical_signs: Loss of deep tendon reflexes (early toxicity)
monitoring.antidote: Calcium Gluconate 10 ml of 10% IV over 10 minutes if toxicity suspected (10meq/L threshold)

indications: Refractory hypokalemia (Mg needed for K correction)

general_guidelines: Given in shock states ONLY — after or concurrent with fluid resuscitation
Prefer central venous catheter; low dose peripheral IV acceptable temporarily
Via large-bore peripheral IV, ensure patent and in proper position
Run on DEDICATED separate IV line
Dilute in D5% ONLY (not NaCl — inactivation risk)
Noradrenaline has very short T½ — bag must be replaced immediately when finishing

steps: Remove 4 ml from a D5% 100 ml bag
Add 1 ampoule of Noradrenaline (4 mg = 4 ml) into the bag

administration.titration: Per physician order, titrate to MAP ≥65 mmHg
administration.monitoring: Continuous BP monitoring (arterial line preferred). Rate changes per physician order only.

protocols.safety: HIGH-RISK DRUG — requires double-check by additional nurse. Monitor for extravasation (tissue necrosis risk).

maintenance.note: Per physician decision and written order

notes: Phlebitis risk with peripheral IV — standard dilution in 500 ml recommended
For volume-restricted patients, may concentrate in 50 ml (requires physician written order for different dilution)
contraindications: AV block (2nd or 3rd degree without pacemaker)
protocols.safety: HIGH-RISK DRUG — requires double-check by additional nurse.

peripheral_iv.diluent_selection: Depends on patient's serum sodium level

central_line.diluent_selection: Depends on patient's serum sodium level

protocols.safety: HIGH-RISK DRUG — requires double-check by additional nurse.
